programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Joe Swash and The Batch Lady transform more families mealtimes. In Yorkshire, can working mum Fiona reduce her food waste and diversify her protein-powered sons diets?

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: The series that celebrates the beautiful south west returns. Cornwalls Lost Gardens of Heligan sow the seeds of a wildflower revolution and we meet Brixhams maverick skipper.

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The potters make body part pots and sculptures of their bodies in motion to fire in self-built brick kilns. Whose fired-up figures will carve them a place in the semi-final?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: The team go to Southend to pick up a 1978 JCB digger. The vehicle belongs to former carpenter Kev, who suffered two heart attacks and is unable to look after his pride and joy.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike gambles on a 1935 Auburn thats either a diamond in the rough or a total dud, the guys tread dangerous ground while picking an Iowa Harley enthusiast, Dave unveils a project one year in the making thats also Mikes biggest-ever investment.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet motocross fan Tom. Henry upcycles two Porsche wheel rims, while Simon makes an extraordinary lamp from old copper piping.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Win the Dream
programme.desc: In a flurry to the finish line, the bespoke bedrooms are completed. And Max drops a dramatic twist ahead of the final that could change everything.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps Jacqui and Michael hunt for a holiday home in Abruzzo in Italy. Theyve always dreamed of finding a home in a mountain village that one day could become a permanent home. Craig has five great properties for Jacquis £70,000 budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sally and Lee see what treats Tenerife has to offer on Costa Adeje for £450k, from a place that would leave you with a tan like a zebra to a gorgeous Moorish duplex giving paradise vibes.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day one of the competition in east London. Fashionista Simran Samra hosts a glitz and glam night for her guests as she goes head to head with doctor Chintu Gademsetty, comedian Yoshki Greenberg, city boy Alan Price and businesswoman Delia Elliot. Simran cooks meat despite being a vegetarian and buys wine despite the fact she knows nothing about it, and her main course isnt a success.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day two in east London, laid-back comedian Yoshki Greenberg aims to throw a relaxing dinner party and give her guests a good laugh, but Yoshki has a surprise up her sleeve: on arrival she hands her guests animal-themed pyjama suits. Sadly not all the guests are put at ease by wearing somebody elses clothes, especially as they havent even been washed.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day three in east London and jack-the-lad Alan Price aims to wow his guests with a medieval themed feast. After two nights of disappointing food, Alan hopes he can raise the standard this week with an ambitious menu, but his dinner party doesnt get off to the best start when his medieval table decoration, a whole pigs head, sends vegetarian Simran in to a state of panic.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day four in east London its the turn of bar owner Delia Elliot to host. Cordon Bleu cook Delia has purposefully played down her menu in an attempt to wow her guests. Despite this, expectations are high. As the elder of the group, Delias confident she can teach her young diners a thing or two about how to throw a good dinner party. However not all the youngsters want to listen.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in east London doctor Chintu Gademsetty is confident of winning with his 80s-themed dinner party, but first he needs to create a cheery atmosphere following the tensions of the past two nights, and secondly he needs to out-do Delias Cordon Bleu cookery. The night begins well when Chintu delights with his MC Hammer costume and Knight Rider car, but who will be the ultimate winner?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Nicola have finally decided to buy a holiday home in the seaside town of Puerto del Carmen on Lanzarote. Ben Hillman has five excellent options for their budget of £180,000.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of first-time buyers search in two of Londons most expensive locations, with Claire on the South Bank, and Geri and Arran in Crouch End.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil meet Heather and Tim in Lothianburn, south of Edinburgh, where their seven-acre property is causing conflict because of its dysfunctional layout.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Joe Swash and The Batch Lady transform more families mealtimes. In Yorkshire, can working mum Fiona reduce her food waste and diversify her protein-powered sons diets?

programme.title: Dog
programme.desc: With a dog named Lulu by his side, Army Ranger Briggs races down the Pacific Coast to make it to a soldiers funeral on time. Along the way, Briggs and Lulu drive each other completely crazy, break a handful of laws and narrowly evade death.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Dirty Business
programme.desc: In an idyllic Oxfordshire hamlet, the fish in the river keep dying. Why? David Thewlis and Jason Watkins lead this real-life drama of victims, whistleblowers and Englands water companies.

programme.title: Dirty Business
programme.desc: Two amateur detectives in an Oxfordshire village discover dying fish in their river. When they contact the water company for answers, the companys odd, evasive response prompts them to launch an investigation that continues today.

programme.title: Dirty Business
programme.desc: Two amateur detectives in an Oxfordshire village discover dying fish in their river. When they contact the water company for answers, the companys odd, evasive response prompts them to launch an investigation that continues today.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: On the beat with Brightons hectic nightlife as trouble comes thick and fast for the night coppers - from a bunch of youths in a street fight to a knocked-out football fan.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Vehicles in need of recovery include a Land Rover Discovery dumped in a canal, a lorry that has lost traction on a steep gradient, and a truck thats nosedived into a domestic back garden.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: When a dog handler is taken out of the chase, multiple cops and the police chopper take up the hunt for a driver who flees on foot.

programme.title: Trucking Heavy
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare a massive demolition machine needs to be dismantled and then transported on five separate trucks to a disused power station 240 miles away in North Yorkshire.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britains roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko and the firearms unit flush out a suspect after finding a homemade gun, while a 999 call leads to a drugs bust for Liam, and dog handler Justin sniffs out the truth when a mum and son tell some porkies over who a crashed a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko and the Firearms team are called to house where a suspected shotgun has been seen, after a short stand-off, the situation soon descends into something more sinister when the team discover what could be a home-made bomb.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: At night, on the A1 in Newcastle, PCs Jan Simlesa and Marc Crammond spot a driver with his foot down, weaving in and out of lanes and bullying other motorists out of his way. In Sunderland, PCs are on the hunt for a BMW driver with no insurance.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.sub-title: Theres No Guarantee, but Theres Skill
programme.desc: With road restrictions and spring break-up on the horizon, $1 million worth of timber could be stranded unless Craig figures out a plan. Brent is behind on logging and clearing a campsite and avoids catastrophe while uprooting a giant tree.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: To escape two corrupt drug-dealing cops in Baltimore, local drug dealer Davon Mayer goes undercover to collect evidence for the FBI.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Wagga Truck Towing rescue a 22-tonne animal truck. Claytons Towing salvage half-a-million-dollars worth of classic cars. Parks Towing take on two emergency missions back-to-back.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Art Burke attempts to keep his rig above ground on a melting ice crossing. Polar driver Alex Debogorski battles his way up a mud covered mountain.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Just outside Newcastle, Kev is sent to rescue a wheelie bin delivery truck that has got stuck. With torrential rain forecast, this job is turning into a recovery nightmare.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Just outside Newcastle, Kev is sent to rescue a wheelie bin delivery truck that has got stuck. With torrential rain forecast, this job is turning into a recovery nightmare.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Following the men and women of the heavy recovery business. In Hertfordshire, rookie op Euan is tasked with rescuing a ten-tonne rigid - one wrong move could end his rescue mission.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Four joyriders have a lucky escape from the wreckage of a Ford Mondeo, stolen property is found in the back of a vehicle and the interceptors have to rush to stop a suicidal man from jumping on to the M25.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Police race to intercept three men suspected of robbing a string of plumbers merchants. A fleeing van speeds the wrong way round a major roundabout and causes a huge crash. Police investigate illegally customised motors in Basildon.

programme.title: The Vanishings
programme.desc: Lisa has to choose who to save: Susan or Mandy? Chris learns new information that takes Lisa to a strange address, where she discovers incriminating information that someone tries to cover up. The killer calls later that evening for Lisas decision.

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in the Sun

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers stop a driver concerning an alleged drunken assault, and the breathalyser indicates he is more than three times over the limit and two knives are found in his car. Elsewhere, Scott is patrolling Peak District villages.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The officers intercept three men armed with a burglary toolkit and apprehend a suspected drug-driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers contend with riotous World Cup revellers, an offender who has breached his ASBO and a shifty suspected drug dealer. Elsewhere, cops are forced to close the Dartford Crossing in order to halt a stolen vehicle.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The cops are on the trail of a group of thieves who targeted a supermarket, a rude biker finds himself the subject of police attention and a disqualified driver tries to evade the long arm of the law.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: An employees compensation claim for an injury at work falls flat. Social media delivers the knockout blow to a martial arts fighters bogus personal injury claim.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Following the residents of the small beach town of Summer Bay, as they deal with relationships, family, love, loss, and growing up.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Following the residents of the small beach town of Summer Bay, as they deal with relationships, family, love, loss, and growing up.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Philip, who has a heart condition, collapsed at home and has been vomiting for the last 10 days. Meanwhile, nurse Chantelle and advanced nurse practitioner Cheryl Barnes work together.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentaries going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the everyday lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals cant.

programme.title: AandE After Dark
programme.desc: The night staff at Hull Royal Infirmary battle to save the life of a patient in cardiac arrest. Dr Chris and nurse Vicky treat a 69-year old fisherman brought into the hospital via a coastguard helicopter.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: A motorist is stuck in his car and unable to move his legs, a man in a hard-to-reach rural location has cut his leg with an angle grinder, and an 80-year-old woman has fallen down the stairs at home and is struggling to breathe.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Dr Dev Shah struggles to treat a football fan who has a painful ball of fat below his ribcage. Dr Emma McMullen deals with a large liquid-filled neck cyst.

programme.title: Call the Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: In London, Scott and police officers remove squatters from a council-owned building where theyve been living for nearly a year. But one woman barricades herself in a room.

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A couple from Kent face losing their house because of a nightmare tenant, and a residential home is found to have been turned into a brothel.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Amir Khan removes the biggest lipoma he has ever seen from Nigels chest, then lances a teenagers blood-blistered fingertip. Vanda was told she had only two years to live, but Dr Chris Harris offers a more positive prognosis.

programme.title: The Vanishings
programme.desc: David and Brennan target Gough as the potential killer whilst Lisa tries to connect him to the kidnappings. Gill threatens to blackmail David if he continues his investigation of Gough. Lisa faces her worst fear with a hospital call about her father.

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: The Eggheads team are made up of some of Britains top quizzers. They face a team of challengers trying to beat the incumbents in order to win a cash prize. If the Eggheads beat the challengers, the cash prize is rolled over to subsequent episodes.

programme.desc: The team pulls over a very angry driver, a car thief leads the officers on a cross-county chase, and the Air Support Unit has its work cut out pursuing an athletic burglar.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drunk driver leads the interceptors on a merry-go-round. The team joins forces with the chopper to track down a wanted criminal on a motorbike, and a young driver crashes straight into an unmarked police car.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Elderly patient Elizabeth is suffering with persistent bleeding, a potential sign of cancer.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: With Barnsley Casualty now declared a crime scene by police, the team have to deal with emergencies both inside and outside the hospital. Doctor Dave needs to negotiate with the police.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: A woman has fallen 50 metres down a waterfall. She has multiple obvious injuries that need urgent care. A man arrives in a critical condition: hes unconscious and his eyes are not reacting to the light.

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out
programme.desc: A knife fight leads to a suspect headbutting a windscreen, theres a suspected terrorist bomb at a shopping centre, and a car colliding with a teegraph pole brings down a live wire.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Dr Toby Nelson checks a patients back and finds a pre-cancerous mole. Plus, Dr Emma McMullen inspects a lipoma on a patients buttock, and Dr Dev Shah treats a collection of skin tags.

programme.desc: A retired couple pay the price when their tenants stop paying rent and invite their abusive friends in to squat.

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Papi Gudia
programme.desc: Before dying, a black magic practicer transfers his soul into a doll which finds its way into the hands of Karishma Kapoors brother. The evil doll has plans of his to get into a new body.

programme.title: Zindagi Gulzar Hai
programme.desc: Nandini, Kishore, Mahesh, Scud, Bhakti, Jessica, Chandana and Sridhar are college friends. Once they graduate, they start focussing on their careers. However, there is more to their life than that.

programme.title: Pyar Ki Kahani
programme.desc: Ram Chand, working a simple job despite his qualifications, meets Ravi Chand who is on the verge of suicide. Convincing him to stay alive and then move in with him, Ram helps Ravi find a job, which in turn finds him a wife. When Ram himself finds what he thinks may be a suitable wife he sends a picture to Ravi for approval, but he reproaches him over his choice and is elusive in his reasons why.

programme.title: Aulad
programme.desc: Believing his wife to be dead, a man lets a woman nurse and bring up his infant son - until he finds out that his wife is still alive, and wants the baby back.

programme.title: Antim Nyay
programme.desc: Jay Singh lives a middle-classed lifestyle along with his dad, Raghunath, and mom, in Bombay. His dad is killed, and both he and his mother attempt to get justice, but their efforts prove to be all in vain.

programme.title: Apartment
programme.desc: A woman throws out her boyfriend when he starts looking at other women and takes on a female lodger, but theres more to her than meets the eye.

programme.title: Laila
programme.desc: Two feuding families attempt to reconcile their differences at a birthday party but fail, resulting in more deaths and one father sending his son away to America to study in peace.

programme.title: Haisiyat
programme.desc: In a Bombay mill the industrious young union leader, Ram, falls in love with the new owner, Sita, and marries her. Ram has been a diligent union boss, working successfully to improve conditions for the workers. After his marriage to the owner, conflicts begin to arise between management and the workforce, and Ram is forced to choose between his love and his honour.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Tha Bing mì-thoilichte g eil Charlie ro bheag airson an geama aige a chluich cuide ris. Bing is frustrated that Charlie is too little to play the game that he has chosen for them.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach
programme.desc: With the moon growing thinner and the woods growing darker night by night, Owl begins to feel lonely and starts to dread his duties. Luckily, Hare has a plan!

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: We hear from a Blue Dragon Fish today.

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh and Tilidh
programme.desc: It is a hot and sunny day on the island, so Abney and Teal make themselves some shady straw hats. Then the weather turns and a strong wind starts blowing them into all sorts of trouble.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.sub-title: Lorgaidh mi an ceangal eadarainn uile!
programme.desc: For homework, Petit and his friends must draw what they imagine the prehistoric missing link looks like. But the kids end up acting like cavemen at Petits house.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Mairi Morrison reads Cuimhnich a-nis, Na Dèan Mèaran, written by Helene Boudreau, illustrated by Serge Bloch and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod.

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Tha seann teampall falaichte le ìomhaighean annasach na dhìomhaireas do Na Neachdan an-diugh. Às bi dè eile, an aire air na freiceadanan!

programme.title: An Sgoil
programme.desc: Tha bhìoras coimpiutaireach mu sgaoil ann an Sgoil Taobh na Cloiche. A computer virus is spreading through Sgoil Taobh na Cloiche.

programme.title: Coig Criomagan
programme.desc: Ellen Macdonald introduces a selection of her favourite facts.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.sub-title: Na Drathaisean Dàna/Beware of the Overpants
programme.desc: Daves lazy day of doing nothing is ruined when the town sees an uprising of their pants.

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: A Przewalskis horse gets castrated, cats and dogs in Barra and surgery on a retriever.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Professor Rob Ó Maolalaigh chats with Joy in the studio about dialects. Calum Maclean visits Islay and learns more about the islands distinctive Gaelic.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Rathad Ramsay
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon is in Dunure in South Ayrshire for a coastal walk in the company of journalist and poet Niall OGallagher. Tha Ramsay MacMahon air chuairt ann an Siorrachd Àir a Deas le Niall OGallagher.

programme.title: Cumhachd Hitler
programme.desc: Hitler had outwardly emphasised his will for peace, yet once he came to power, he committed his top commanders to a war of extermination that would secure the German Reichs supremacy in Europe and so-called `Living Space in the East.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Tha Abigail ag ullachadh Bircher Muesli, bracaist làn spionnaidh. Abigail prepares a Bircher muesli, perfect for a hearty breakfast.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotlands Wild Side
programme.desc: Coinneach MacFhraing pits himself against difficult winter conditions in one of Scotlands fearsome mountain environments.

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Topical affairs programme reporting the major political, social, and cultural issues across Europe.

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Paul Heaton le aoigh Rianne Downey a toirt a chiad oidhche gu ceann aig Belladrum 2025.

programme.title: OMC!
programme.desc: An all-new sketch show, featuring an intimate dining experience, an embarrassing confrontation in the shopping aisle, and old friends at war.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Castles: Britains Fortified History
programme.desc: Sam Willis explores how by the Wars of the Roses castles were under attack from a new threat - the cannon, but survived into the Tudor era only to find their whole purpose challenged.

programme.title: This Is BBC Four
programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor looks at how the nations canal network was saved following years of neglect and finds out how Birmingham led the way in urban regeneration, turning canals and their environs from places to be avoided, to places to embrace.

programme.title: Gareth Edwards Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Maureen has arranged a trip the spectacular Brecon Beacons. But its not the usual walks amid epic mountain scenery on the agenda. In a first for both, the childhood sweethearts enlist in tank school. After donning cam cream and military gear, the couple get a lesson in controlling a full-sized tank.

programme.title: The Bridge: Fifty Years Across The Forth
programme.desc: A unique amateur film provides the centrepiece of a documentary celebrating the 50th anniversary of one of Scotlands great landmarks, the Forth Road Bridge.

programme.title: The Read
programme.sub-title: The 39 Steps: The Read with John Hannah
programme.desc: BAFTA-nominated actor John Hannah gives a thrilling reading performance of this classic text. Murder, conspiracy and espionage are the themes of John Buchans gripping novel, set against the backdrop of WWI, that sees Richard Hannay, a bored adventurer, become embroiled in a plot to assassinate a political figure.

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Richard Hannay, pursued by the police for a murder he did not commit, and by an international spy-ring for information he does not possess, finds himself fleeing across the desolate Scottish moors - handcuffed to a beautiful blonde.

programme.title: Calendar Girls
programme.desc: Based on the true story of 12 ladies from Yorkshire baring all for charity. When one of the womens husbands dies from leukaemia, they decide that their prim and proper fundraising efforts need a bit of a shake-up. They find a more compelling way to raise money - by starring in a nude calendar. Their modest hopes of selling a few hundred copies around the village are outstripped when it becomes a worldwide sensation.

programme.title: The Horizon Guide to AI
programme.desc: The BBCs Horizon programme began in 1964, and since then has produced films looking at computer technology and the emergence of `artificial intelligence. The dreams always begin with ideology and optimism, only for this optimism to be replaced with suspicion that AI machines will take over. However, as the archive shows, throughout each decade once people have learnt to live with the new emerging technology of the time, the pattern begins again.

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor looks at how the nations canal network was saved following years of neglect and finds out how Birmingham led the way in urban regeneration, turning canals and their environs from places to be avoided, to places to embrace.

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: In summer 1939, on the cusp of World War Two, explorers discovered three astonishing ancient treasures that would transform our understanding of the origins of civilisation.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Kites and Koras on the Cosmopolitan Coast
programme.desc: Its a cosmopolitan end to dapper duo Izzie Balmer and Mark Hills trip as they sweep along the Sussex coast in search of treasures. Izzie is dazzled by a vast haul of Chinese kites, while Mark hopes that an out-of-fashion etching could be an auction sleeper. They pause their shopping in vibrant Brighton to help make a kora, the traditional instrument of west African storytellers, before hearing a maestro perform. But with the final auction looming, who will hit all the right notes?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and JB Gill bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: This weeks quarter-finalists are faced with an invention test that must scream chicken, but which only includes off-cuts.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is in the hosts chair of the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists David Morrissey, Holly Willoughby, John Kearns and Montell Douglas.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team is challenged by a perplexing poisoning during a performance of The Tempest. Meanwhile, Mervin must decide what to do about a difficult development involving his brother.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: First of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring a host of stars, including Dwayne Johnson, Emily Blunt, Matthew McConaughey, Taylor Swift and many more.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Silence is golden as the queens tackle an acting challenge with a difference. Split into two teams, the queens must let their bodies do the talking as they star in silent movies, with direction from Doctor Who supremo Russell T Davies. Acting superstar Will Poulter joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Alan Carr. May the best drag queen win!

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: Dr Don Shirley is a world-class African-American pianist, who is about to embark on a concert tour in the Deep South in 1962. In need of a driver and protection, Shirley recruits Tony Lip, a tough-talking bouncer from an Italian-American neighbourhood in the Bronx. Despite their differences, the two men soon develop an unexpected bond while confronting racism and danger in an era of segregation.

programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Dr Xands Con or Cure
programme.desc: With weight-loss jabs in high demand, criminals are flooding the market with deadly cheap substitutes. Also on the programme, the growing trend of people using a tobacco-free pouch seen as less harmful than vaping or smoking, but which is dangerously addictive. And Dr Xand explores the idea that a full moon affects our behaviour.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A firefighter is on hand to try to save a Somerset terrace. Owain is taken by the period features in a Worcestershire house. And we catch up with a plot of land in Staffordshire first seen in 2022.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is in Cheshire with experts Charles Hanson and Caroline Hawley as the teams go shopping for antiques to sell at auction in Stockport. Eric takes a trip to Cuckooland Museum to find out about a huge collection of over 700 cuckoo clocks.

programme.desc: Its a cosmopolitan end to dapper duo Izzie Balmer and Mark Hills trip as they sweep along the Sussex coast in search of treasures. Izzie is dazzled by a vast haul of Chinese kites, while Mark hopes that an out-of-fashion etching could be an auction sleeper. They pause their shopping in vibrant Brighton to help make a kora, the traditional instrument of west African storytellers, before hearing a maestro perform. But with the final auction looming, who will hit all the right notes?

Acting superstar Will Poulter joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Alan Carr. May the best drag queen win!

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: Dr Don Shirley is a world-class African-American pianist, who is about to embark on a concert tour in the Deep South in 1962. In need of a driver and protection, Shirley recruits Tony Lip, a tough-talking bouncer from an Italian-American neighbourhood in the Bronx. Despite their differences, the two men soon develop an unexpected bond while confronting racism and danger in an era of segregation.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and his team take to the waters of British Columbia in search of more monsters of the deep seas. Steve tracks the weirdest looking fish on the planet, armed with chisel-like teeth, comes face to face with a giant Pacific octopus and gets the surprise of his life when he goes kayaking with killer whales.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes to the icy waters of Vancouver in search of the biggest, baddest sea lion in the world. After an unexpected encounter with a killer whale, he heads back to the city on the trail of one smelly contender before trying his hand at becoming a raccoon. Despite immense strength, powerful claws and a fearsome reputation in Canada, theres a surprise in store.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May confront the credit crunch head on as they attempt to find three sensibly priced small cars that wont make you miserable, a process that involves haranguing bankers, harnessing large dogs, and almost getting arrested. Plus, James meets American stunt driving legend Ken Block, and Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz finds Donnas toothbrush in his flat and realises he has to stop her merging, and Janets plan to make Jonny fancy her backfires.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Johnny falls for Janets friend Louise, until his obsession is cured in a revolting manner, Janet decides to become a stand-up comedian, and Gaz attempts to manipulate Donna.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps

programme.desc: The British people are proud of their beef dishes, and the French are too. Steak frites is a national institution, but in Provence, Marcus is expecting something a little different.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When the Farmer decides he and Bitzer should head out to spend some time together, Shaun seizes his chance to take charge. He soon discovers the job is not quite as easy as he first thought.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Other Day the Earth Stood Still
programme.desc: Only Danger Mouse can save the world from chaos when the Baron stops the world spinning with a plague of traffic lights.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: An angry Swampclops follows the heroes back from their quest for a giant raspberry, so Hoofius must abandon his chances of winning the bake-off.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The family is going on a canoe trip, and Boy and Girl are each allowed to bring a friend. For Girl, the choice is easy - she invites her BFF. But Boy realizes he doesnt really have any friends outside his own family. The rest of the siblings set out to help Boy find a friend for the canoe trip - but can he overcome his shyness?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have organised a mega party at the cabin. This is obviously not to Grizzys liking and the bear throws them out. The Lemmings discover a cave where the party is even more fun, but their ruckus drives out the bats.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob helps out the Krusty Krabs night shift crew, but the customers crave something creepier.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Olive and Otto interrogate two suspects to figure out which one is innocent and which one is guilty.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Danny and Mick are waiting in reception for a package to be delivered. While they wait, they reminisce about the hilarious things they have been through together.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.sub-title: You Could Run at the Speed of Sound?
programme.desc: A fun run goes wrong when one ambitious runner tries running at the speed of sound.

programme.title: Bitesize: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about animal babies, eggs and big changes.

programme.title: Wow Thats Amazing
programme.desc: Get ready for incredible experiments and mind-bending puzzles. Owain and Tilly are here to explore epic science tricks to try at home and showcase new technology innovations from around the world.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Alex Scott joins Rattus for a special sport-themed show, marking the 2020 Olympics. The original Olympics werent actually much to do with sport at all, and so our show begins in Ancient Greece, where the Festival Committee is looking to pep-up its Religious Festival celebrating Zeus with the inclusion of some new events? and who better to help them figure out what those events should be than Sophie and Sebastian?! Did you know that sport has been banned at various times in History?

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky finds himself in a bit of a fairy tale when his latest tech toy TESA (Technologically Encrypted Support Assistant) decides to become an audio book. Things get even weirder when Sarah transforms into the wicked witch of the west country, and Gav shows up with three little pigs. So when they all disappear in Bristol city centre, Ricky finds himself part of an epic art adventure. Ricky makes dragon feet trainers and a beastly collage inspired by artist Hannah Hoch.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew are searching for elusive giant river otters and there is a chance encounter with the largest reptile in South America. Nothing prepares Steve for the animal that takes the last place on the `Deadly 60 list.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: In todays show, the doctors look at mucus and show you how to make snot rockets!

programme.title: Absolute Genius With Dick and Dom
programme.desc: Dick and Dom reveal the genius of Michael Faraday, a pioneer of electricity whose discoveries helped power the planet. Inspired by Faradays genius ideas, Dick and Dom come up with their own genius idea, involving a cage, and almost a million volts of electricity.

programme.title: Bros in Control
programme.desc: Callum sets up the ultimate Grand Prix, and real F1 drivers advise Joe on how to win.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks

programme.desc: A robot, rockstar, broccoli or water over the head - the Gladiators dish out the dares!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts find the fossil of a prehistoric fish while exploring a dark and creepy cave.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard go paddleboarding, but Dunk and the river creatures are behaving oddly. Could it be something to do with Dads desire for a cup of tea?

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris find a skeleton and believe its an ancestor - but all is not what it appears.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are made to clean the Cave of Mirrors as punishment for frolicking before a quest is complete, and they accidentally release the Party Harpy.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes set out on a quest to find out who is draining colour from the forest and muster all their positivity to save it from greyness.

programme.desc: Sulas cousin Nicky is visiting, something Bing is a little unsure about. programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels stumble upon a room full of old radio equipment and discover that Duggee used to host his own show! Duggee reminisces about his radio days running Clubhouse Radio, playing music, telling jokes, chatting to callers and reading the news. It was the most popular show on air, until a new rival show appeared and replaced him: the tellingly-named Cheese Radio.

programme.desc: While waiting to be picked up from school, Jack plays at being a sailor trying to find his way back home to Australia. Jacks dad, however, has got lost en route to collect him.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Squirrel Hotpot
programme.desc: When Mr Tod catches Squirrel Nutkin and takes him to Owl Island to get a squirrel recipe from Old Brown, it falls to Peter, Lily, Benjamin and the other squirrels to save Nutkin from being turned into a squirrel hotpot dinner!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The pull of a strange planet splits the Numberblocks into hundreds, tens and units!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: B the bassist and her brother D the drummer get new guitarist N to join the band, but they still need a singer. They try auditioning the vowels but make some strange sounds before the band is complete.

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: The tide is out, and theres lots to see as the Noggle lands on the seashore. There must be a Tiny Wonder to explore. Yapp-Yapp makes a discovery that surprises all the Nogglins.

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: Flex and bend into my ExtraStretchy Challenge. Stretch your skills with arm circles, lunges and toe taps, then get flexible in the Activate.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift have fun playing with their favourite red bouncy ball. It bounces into the Ice Disco, where it gives Hettie the Yeti an idea for a bouncy dance!

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the rock `n roll star Parsley performing Rise Up with Me about all things rock.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Its bedtime, but wheres Hoppity? Bing remembers playing with him in the garden but now its all dark out there and he doesnt really like the dark. Flop finds Bings carrot torch and together they set off into the dark on an adventure.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `scooter, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different scooters, a purple scooter, a blue scooter and a bopping scooter. Theres a new scooter, a toot your hooter scooter and a family on scooters.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Declan gets carried away when hes put in charge of an all-powerful flower. Barnaby gets scared of the big horseflies and bonds with a baby one. The bugs help a new bug to feel welcome in Shell School. The bugs talk about nature.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies put on a big concert so that all of Fine Forest can hear Cherrys amazing voice. They build a huge stage, and everything is almost ready - but Cherry has no song to sing!

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a warehouse, underneath a cardboard box. They watch a man load a lorry with parcels, but then he goes to load the box which the Twirlywoos are hiding underneath. The box runs around the warehouse and the man tries to catch it.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of `Tumbletons Got Talent starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pretend to fly. Po goes so fast on the scooter that it takes off and flies over Teletubbyland! The children in Tummy Tales watch swans flying.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oakley, Arthur, Luna, Reau, Tylana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside Giovannas bag is a toy camera, and all the parents and babies have objects and toys that are themed around a day out.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a farm with her friend Hugo and plays a game of I Spy.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: While playing at the beach, Maddie and Triggs try to save their sandcastle from the incoming tide, in order to provide a fancy home for their magical new friend, Sailor Nancy.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan imagines hes a construction worker wants to build the tallest building ever. When the finished structure doesnt work out as planned, he needs to think outside the (cardboard) box.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Panias Uncle Tama is very old and seems ready for a sleep.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Chloe embark on a quest to save their playground kingdom from the Greedy Queen.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tag is feeling grumpy because he didnt eat his breakfast. Luckily, Duggee has his Breakfast Badge, so he can make whatever the Squirrels would like to eat - and it turns out they all like very different things for breakfast! The Squirrels learn all about how different animals fuel themselves in the mornings, earning their Breakfast Badges in the process. Tag finally gets his toast, helping power him through the day. Well done Squirrels!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The class are playing shop. Timmy and Finlay both have their hearts set on a fantastic toy plane, but neither has enough buttons to buy it. They set up rival shops and try to raise more buttons, until they realise that working together is better than rivalry.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Mum and Dad have a treat in store for George and Freddie. Its a special tennis game including dinosaurs, bees, and smiley faces. Later, George and Freddie get the face paints out.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Elsa is using salt fish to make a fishcake recipe she has learnt from her father. She starts by mashing potatoes with butter before adding lots of other tasty ingredients such as chives. Not everyone likes the taste of chives before they are cooked but hopefully they will enjoy them in the finished recipe. When her friends arrive, Elsa greets them with a special pineapple drink served in pineapple-shaped cups.

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Bella has lots of questions about some very special beetles, lovely ladybirds. Ferne meets some sun beetles, horned flour beetles and purple jewel beetles at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy look for ladybirds in the greenhouse.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Its the Woofton Wolves last football match of the season, and Rayane is in danger of missing the second half. Hearing dog Diesel helps Rayane at his teams last football match of the season.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Owl Butterfly and Sunbittern
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie head into the heat of the butterfly house to meet the owl butterfly. They discover that this giant butterfly gets its name from the distinct patterns on its wings, which look like an owls eye. Next, they visit the sunbittern.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie forgets her reading book, so Waffle takes it to her at school. As Waffle reaches the playground, he sees Evies P.E. Class and is desperate to join in.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Baba finds a beautiful, shiny shell that causes some seagull trouble for Oona.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The potatoes celebrate the four seasons and sing a folksy song about the exciting things that come with each one.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Twinkle discovers an army of marching ants taking seeds from her vegetable garden. When all attempts to stop them fail, she and Yukee try to find a way to get the ants marching to a different beat and away from her garden.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tino and Eugene disagree about an amateur dramatic show they want to put on. One wants an all-singing, all-dancing extravaganza, while the other wants a serious drama. Can Duggee and the Squirrels help them get something ready?

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say book and go on a whirlwind word adventure. See lots of different books and explore how they look and feel and see a flip book, a flap book and make a scrapbook.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks transport block fruit to Blockzilla and learn about weight using a pair of balancing scales.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks sing along to a singalong song, in which birds sing and bells ring.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a roarsome adventure to a dinosaur park, while Mr Tumble visits Shopkeeper Tumble in `The Shop with the Lot to buy Tumble Ted a birthday present.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies do conga dances over the hills, then they conga into Home Dome to answer the Tubby Phone and do a special conga Tubby Phone Dance. In Tummy Tales, the children have lots of fun as they conga around a soft play centre.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Spider has a problem, he wants to play music on his net but the music sounds bad. The Patchwork Pals find a way to help him.

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Hufftys not feeling well so his friends try to cheer him up with games, but his loud elephant sneezes keep interrupting them. Thats until Raa Raa thinks up a way to make his sneezes part of the fun!

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: When a new chugger, Rosa, comes to Chuggington, the trainees try to find the best job for her.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Cheeky pets join in with the fun. Accident-prone DIY Dan attempts to build a kennel for his little dog whilst flamboyant ballroom dancer Enrico Paso Doble gets distracted by his pet dog.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all kinds of transport. Grace goes underground to drive a tube train, shows off a hanging railway and then drives a hydrogen boat.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on Book Planet to play tower of cups.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When a gigantic saltwater crocodile wanders into freezing waters, the Octonauts must figure out how to get the predator back to his warm water home - without anyone getting hurt.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Lucky Four Leaf Clover
programme.desc: When Benjamin finds a four-leaf clover, Peter tricks him into thinking it brings good luck. Its harmless fun until Peters prank accidentally leads Lily into Mr Tods dangerous clutches.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Happy discovers his wobbly tooth has come out, but hes lost it! Hes having a problem remembering where he was when it fell out. The Squirrels start to look for it but they cant find the tooth anywhere.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Pippen is ready to sing at her concert, until her cold threatens to ruin the performance.

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Its pumpkin-picking day at Jacksons farm, but something spooky has been scaring all the pumpkin pickers. Mojo and Bo need to find out whats up, and chase some ghosts!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While waiting to be picked up from school, Jack plays at being a sailor trying to find his way back home to Australia. Jacks dad, however, has got lost en route to collect him.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A man arrives at AandE with two of his severed fingers in a bag of frozen peas after an accident chopping wood. And pro-bike rider Max has hit his head in a high-speed crash.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Richard is rushed to Queens, Nottingham, after collapsing suddenly at home and may be going into organ failure. And Colin has sliced his hand open with a Stanley knife.

programme.title: Can You Ever Forgive Me?
programme.desc: Lee Israel is a frustrated, hard-drinking author who can barely afford to pay her rent or bills in 1990s New York. Desperate for money, Israel soon hatches a scheme to forge letters by famous writers and sell them to bookstores and collectors. When the dealers start to catch on, Lee recruits a dubious friend to help her continue her self-destructive cycle of trickery and deceit.

programme.desc: Cliff tells Carla that he is one of the judges in the Miss Boston barmaid contest, and that if she wants to win, she must be nice to him, even if it kills her.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam meets his old baseball nemesis, Dutch Kincaid, when he invites Sam to pitch for him at a `Dutch Kincaid Day held at Yankee Stadium.

programme.desc: As Ray escorts Ally to a father-daughter dance, he realises how grown up and independent his daughter has become, and recalls the day when she was born.

programme.desc: After watching a focus group evaluate his show, Frasier obsesses about a newsstand owner who expressed a negative opinion.

programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.desc: Three men get their hopes up on marriage when a beautiful woman moves into their neighbourhood.

programme.title: Maharathi
programme.desc: A street-smart newly recruited chauffeur partners with his late employers wife to pocket a hefty insurance claim.

programme.title: Mann
programme.desc: Priya wins a cruise, where she meets Dev and falls in love with him but, because each is bound to an arranged marriage, they part ways and promise to meet again after a year.

programme.title: Humko Tumse Pyaar Hai
programme.desc: A blind woman struggles to come to terms with her first love, who has apparently been killed, and a new love who wants to help her move on with her life.

programme.title: Soldier
programme.desc: A young man flees India and arrives in Australia seeking vengance for something that started in India.

programme.title: Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani
programme.desc: Flamboyant Punjabi Rocky and intellectual Bengali journalist Rani fall in love despite their differences. After facing family opposition, they decide to live with each others families for three months before getting married.

programme.title: Dhoom
programme.desc: A gang of robbers on hi-tech motorbikes, led by the charismatic Kabir, is sweeping through Mumbai, outwitting the police at every turn. When officer Jai Dixit is assigned to catch the criminals, he reluctantly enlists the help of garage mechanic and bike enthusiast Ali. Though the pair do not see eye-to-eye at first, they soon become a formidable team, tracking their enemies from the streets of Mumbai to the beaches of Goa.

programme.title: Dhoom
programme.desc: A gang of robbers on hi-tech motorbikes, led by the charismatic Kabir, is sweeping through Mumbai, outwitting the police at every turn. When officer Jai Dixit is assigned to catch the criminals, he reluctantly enlists the help of garage mechanic and bike enthusiast Ali. Though the pair do not see eye-to-eye at first, they soon become a formidable team, tracking their enemies from the streets of Mumbai to the beaches of Goa.

programme.title: Bloody Daddy
programme.desc: A messed up and tenacious man faces off against cops and crime lords to save the one relationship that matters to him: the one with his son.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she cant bring herself to do it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk
programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebes husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant
programme.desc: Monica gets promoted and invites the friends out for dinner to celebrate, but problems arise when the difference in their salaries becomes apparent and the group splits in two. Meanwhile, when Chandler sets a woman up to get a date with her, a case of mistaken identities means that he discovers what she really thought of him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachels negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds out that her picture of her father, whom she has never known, is just a generic photograph that comes with the picture frame. Elsewhere, after Ross makes Monicas heater malfunction, her Christmas party guests swelter in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets a great acting opportunity on `Days of our Lives, Rachel dates a guy called Russ who is amazingly similar to Ross, and Monica renews her relationship with `Fun Bobby, but finds out Bobby is only fun when he is drinking.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding
programme.desc: Rachels free-spirited mother visits and reveals that she wants to end her marriage and experiment sexually. Meanwhile, Carol and Susan prepare for their wedding, with some help from an uncomfortable Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Joey starts dating a fan who is gorgeous, but soon discovers that shes also quite mad. Meanwhile, Ross is reminded how much he misses Marcel his old pet monkey, and travels to San Diego Zoo to find out what has happened to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross finally spends some time with Marcel after many false starts, and Chandler is delighted to get a date with the sexy Susie, who he hasnt seen since fourth grade. However, she gets her revenge for youthful humiliation by leaving him in the loo, naked but for a pair of her knickers.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Prom Video
programme.desc: When Monicas parents clear out her old room to install a gym, they unearth an old video of her and Rachels first prom night. Its obvious from this that Ross is in love with Rachel and, having told him it will never happen, Rachel changes her mind. Elsewhere, Joey gives Chandler a hideous bracelet as a token of his friendship and is mortally offended to hear Chandler saying how terrible it is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel ... You Know
programme.desc: Among the guests at Monicas party is a handsome and sophisticated doctor, but the only problem is that hes old enough to be her father. Meanwhile, Joey buys a big screen TV and two reclining leather armchairs with the money from his `Days of Our Lives contract.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Everyone at Monicas dads birthday party finds it amusing that their middle-aged friend, eye-doctor Richard Burke, is dating a woman in her 20s - until Monicas parents learn that it is their daughter. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey row over a dirty spoon. Is the apartment still big enough for both of them?

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II
programme.desc: Mick `Crocodile Dundee, now living in New York, wages war against a drug-running gang when his reporter girlfriend is kidnapped because she possesses incriminating film of a drug-related murder. programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.sub-title: Looking for Luck in All the Right Cases
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

In this first episode, Gordon visits the Hot Potato Cafe in Fishtown, Philadelphia. With no profit made in its two years of trading and an influential restaurant reviewer dubbing them `Spuddy Hell, Gordon soon realises Hot Potato may have had its chips.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural Whitehouse, New Jersey. Mother and daughter owners Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways but their restaurant is far from paradise. They are in deep financial trouble and Adele struggles to get on with her staff, is disappointed by her chef and hates Gordon with a passion. Gordon has one last chance to bring them all around and make the restaurant a success.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly nurses Coach Mellor back to health when he falls off the rope climbing equipment. Erica and her roommate get on each others nerves.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry enlists his friends help to break up Beverlys social group to get more of her attention. Adam attempts to trick Murray into loving the theatre.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire meet Dylans hippy mum, who turns out to have a very interesting past. Then, Cam and Mitch make a discovery in Lilys room.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria plays a trick that gets Phil and Joe in the malls new ad campaign. Elsewhere, technology gets the better of Jay, and Cam faces up to a school bully.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Bazzini restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Owner Paul Bazzini was an award-winning chef in New York who decided to run his own restaurant but he never realised how difficult the change would be. Now, he finds everyday tasks overwhelming and his passion for food is in danger of being extinguished by the daily grind. However, passion isnt the only problem in this failing restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. Owners Marcelo and Kata used to be married to each other but now fight over everything. Seven years ago, the couple were happy newlyweds and the restaurant was their shared dream, but now the passion has gone and theyre constantly at each others throats, with their staff getting caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Holt adjust to their new lives in the Witness Protection Program, while Jimmy `The Butcher Figgis is still at large. Jake and Holts anonymity is at risk when a video of them goes viral.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jimmy `The Butcher Figgis sees the viral video, prompting Jake and Holt to begin plotting their attack. Back at the precinct, a new captain takes over.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Troy McClure hosts three spin-off stories from `The Simpsons television show. Featuring the voice of the late Phil Hartman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
programme.desc: Lisa enrols in an all-male military school and is subjected to challenges and silent treatment by the cadets.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam tells Beverly he doesnt want her to chaperone the school trip to a theme park, his mother tries to manipulate him with `guilt letters.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Jackies parents allow Adam to watch a horror film, a disagreement between the families ensues. Erica hangs out at her old high school, despite the fact that she is no longer a student.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family looks back at how they all celebrated their birthdays over the past year, as the birth of Haley and Dylans twins is approaching.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley is determined to follow the advice in her parenting books, but Phil and Claire think their old methods are better. Manny is set to direct Jays dog bed commercial in hopes of winning his ex-girlfriend back.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When a prison van crashes, a mass escape of convicts takes place and the entire squad must go on a manhunt to track them down. Peralta and Santiago make a bet on which of them can bring in the most escaped prisoners.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Only one fugitive remains on the loose on the streets of Brooklyn after the mass escape of convicts from the prison van and Peralta is forced to enlist a surprising ally in order to apprehend him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer invests all the familys money in a company that makes `Animotion machines. After the company declares bankruptcy, Homer becomes a human guinea pig to raise some cash. The scientists find a crayon lodged in his brain and when it is removed, he suddenly becomes extremely intelligent.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer ends up in the prison infirmary after visiting the prison rodeo, Marge notices the artistic talent of jailbird Jack Crowley and vouches for him at his parole hearing. However, when she gets him a job painting a mural at the school, he clashes artistically with Principal Skinner. Meanwhile, Homer sets up a chiropractor business with the help of an old dustbin, aka the Spine-O-Cylinder.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart and Lisa stop watching Itchy and Scratchy together, Marge fears that theyll start to drift apart.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa signs up for a one-way ticket to Mars, forcing Marge to employ a devious strategy to keep her daughter on Earth.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: The celeb bakers tackle decorated traybakes, a classic British staple and an impressive biscuit scene depicting what they wanted to be when they grew up.

programme.title: Terminator Genisys
programme.desc: When John Connor, leader of the human resistance against Skynet, sends Kyle Reese back to 1984 to protect his mother, Sarah, from a Terminator assassin, an unexpected turn of events creates an altered timeline. Instead of a scared waitress, Sarah is a skilled fighter and has a Terminator guardian by her side. Faced with unlikely allies and dangerous new enemies, Reese sets out on an unexpected new mission: reset the future.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: We celebrate the celibate and the pickers who entered the studio with their virginity very much intact, ready for an on telly awakening like no other.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: We catch up with our most celebrated starkers stars, including 75-year-old bisexual Ian from Series 8, and husband-seeking Shaida from Series 7 who was looking for the father of her future children.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, ladies man Terry is back in the restaurant to find love, hoping it will be third time lucky. Has he met his match with bi-sexual Larisha?

programme.title: Son of Robin Hood
programme.desc: The merry men find themselves a man to lead them once they learn Robin Hoods son is actually a daughter.

programme.title: The Lost World
programme.desc: Thrilling story about a zoology professor who leads an expedition to a high plateau in the Amazon jungle where prehistoric life has survived. programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is in Rome, tracking down the fast, fresh and easy recipes the natives of the buzzing Italian capital city cook every day. He learns how to make tasty minty pea tortiglioni, and gnocchi with roasted tomato sauce and rocket pesto.

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Italy
programme.desc: It is mid-winter and Jamie Oliver is high up in the snow-capped Italian Alps cooking some great comfort food, including dumplings, mind-blowing macaroni cheese, and a classic red wine and sausage risotto.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Si and Dave taste some of Russias finest foods (washed down with a vodka) and go down the rabbit hole of a country and culture we know very little about. What will they think of modern Russia when they come out the other side?

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Taste of Tex Mex
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is serving up her favourite Tex-Mex recipes, starting with Spicy Shrimp and Grits the whole family loves; Chicken and Veggie Sheet Pan Fajitas; Irresistible Chile Lime Cauliflower drizzled with Smokey Mayo; Churro Chip Sundae.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is on the ranch cooking up family favourites with the help of a soon-to-be-married crew! Theres got to be soup -- Tuscan with Chicken, to be exact; casserole with Cheesy Greens, fried Crispy Potato Balls and an Easy Neapolitan Cake.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Sausage and Mash Pie, Fish and Chips
programme.desc: Jamie creates a sausage pie using golden mashed potato instead of pastry, and gives the nations favourite fish and chips a healthy makeover.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver spices up dinner with his take on peri peri chicken, makes fishcakes with sustainable white fish, and turns a simple ice cream into a cheeky treat.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina shares her international pasta recipes, with a baked Italian pasta dish, a Jewish classic sweet and savoury pasta dish, and an Asian pasta salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina shares her formula for the perfect cocktail party, with home made canapes, and tips on setting up a cocktail bar at home.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is the semi-final and the remaining bakers are just three patisserie challenges away from the biggest day in their baking lives. The heat is really on now with the complex Signature, Technical and Showstopper challenges.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve bakers started. Now just three remain but only one can win. Paul and Prue set three challenges that will test the bakers skills, including an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape Showstopper that could win them the title.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: James Martin is one of Britains premier pastry and pudding makers, here he presents some classic French desserts - creme brulee, banana tarte tatin and gateau St. Honore.

programme.desc: James Martin is one of Britains premier pastry and pudding makers, here he focuses on whats known, for obvious reasons, as comfort food - apple pie, rhubarb crumble and custard, spotted dick and sticky toffee pudding.

programme.desc: Twelve bakers started. Now just three remain but only one can win. Paul and Prue set three challenges that will test the bakers skills, including an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape Showstopper that could win them the title.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernseys wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaires yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into some righteous ramen in St. Petersburg, Florida, a funky joint frying up chicken gizzards in Lansing, Michigan, and a New Orleans smokehouse putting out Big Easy favourites like spicy shrimp boudin.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris grabbing standout sandwiches, including a primo pork burger in Longmont, Colorado, and diving into some serious spice with fiery Thai specialities in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris rolling into college towns in search of A+ cuisine. In Columbus, Ohio, he finds a pierogi paradise and a barbecue truck smoking premiere pork. In Boulder, Colorado, he stops at a Thai joint serving knockout noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy digs into a late-night spot all about breakfast in Los Angeles, a funky joint serving creative combos in Columbus, Ohio, and a barbecue spot smokin up pork belly dumplings in Phoenix.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. In Puerto Rico, he tries the Sleeping Cow challenge, attempting to eat a nine pound steak platter in less than 40 minutes.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. His latest food endurance test is the Stupid Wings Challenge: eating eight super spicy wings in 30 minutes or less.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy, Mauro and their families are in Disney World to make cannoli for the Epcot Food and Wine Festival.

programme.title: Cake Boss
