programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Last Frontier of the United States armed with his 1899 Appletons Guide-Book to Alaska. Beginning his journey amid the snow-capped mountains and ice-cold inlets of the Kenai Peninsula, Michael finds the golden onion domes of a Russian Orthodox church along with traditional Russian food and costume and discovers that, 150 years ago, Alaska was a Russian colony.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1899 Appletons Guidebook to Alaska, Michael Portillo rides the Alaska Railroad north to explore the remote former gold rush settlement of Talkeetna. Deep in the forest outside town, Michael gets a taste of the pioneering spirit of early 20th century prospectors and settlers from a modern day `homesteader and helps fell a tree to clear land for a log cabin.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves on his way to Grimsby to pick up an iconic 80s fast Ford, the Mk1 Fiesta XR2.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek gets a tour of Hadlow Castle in Kent when he collects an immaculate Citroen that was once used by the Gestapo in occupied France, Dave considers spending his own money on a hay cart, and Paul unearths a remarkably rust-free 1970s Alfa GTV.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated with the Resistance to hinder the Nazis use of military vehicles.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear how the arrival of American forces and an Allied counteroffensive in 1918 helped defeat Germany. Tragically, 11,000 men were killed or injured on Armistice Day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Cardiff Castle. In this Victorian gothic setting, silver specialist Gordon Foster is fascinated by an elaborately decorated Indian flask, while John Benjamin reveals that what was thought to be costume jewellery is actually the real thing. Fiona is on the hunt for a lost 1933 penny worth a small fortune and Henry Sandon gets his hands on a piece of rare porcelain which he has been waiting 40 years to see.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team sets up camp outside the iconic Parliament Building in Belfast, home to the Northern Ireland Assembly. Items featured include the walking stick of Lord Haw-Haw, the traitor executed for helping the Nazis during WWII. Also, John Baddeley takes a look at some gear associated with Titanic, and can Fiona determine the changing fortunes of three books from literary masters?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated with the Resistance to hinder the Nazis use of military vehicles.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear how the arrival of American forces and an Allied counteroffensive in 1918 helped defeat Germany. Tragically, 11,000 men were killed or injured on Armistice Day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Cardiff Castle. In this Victorian gothic setting, silver specialist Gordon Foster is fascinated by an elaborately decorated Indian flask, while John Benjamin reveals that what was thought to be costume jewellery is actually the real thing. Fiona is on the hunt for a lost 1933 penny worth a small fortune and Henry Sandon gets his hands on a piece of rare porcelain which he has been waiting 40 years to see.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Starting at the spectacular Giants Causeway in Northern Ireland, Simon travels down the east coast to the great and rapidly changing cities of Belfast and Dublin. He ends his journey in the stunning Wicklow Mountains.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: MG Magnette; Morris Minor; De Tomaso Lomngchamp; Ford Escort Rally Car
programme.desc: Derek collects a pair of stylish British classics from a Yorkshire workshop, with the Morris 1000 finding a new home with a 10-year-old superfan.

programme.title: Hunting Outback Gold
programme.desc: Hot on the trail of a lost fortune in gold, Andrew and Jeff find a hidden location where legendary explorer Lasseter may have camped.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a crumbling dam hidden in the Welsh valleys, a quaint outpost on a paradise island, and a mastery of ancient engineering that brought life to the planes of Mexico.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a complex in Georgia pointing into deep space that almost discovered a signal from aliens.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated with the Resistance to hinder the Nazis use of military vehicles.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a second spectacular rail journey through Alaska into Canada on the White Pass and Yukon railway. Arriving in Skagway by seaplane from railwayless Juneau, Michael heads first for Dyea and the Chilkoot trail, which the first gold prospectors hiked a hundred years ago to the Klondike. Among them, he discovers, was author, Jack London, whose stories of sled dogs captured the spirit of the gold rush.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores British Columbia, steered by his Appletons Guide to Canada, published in 1899. He discovers how two superpowers nearly came to war over a pig and joins the Royal Canadian Navy to firefight on board the frigate HMCS Regina. Starting on Vancouver Island, Michael explores the rich British heritage and colonial past of the provincial capital of British Columbia, Victoria.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family continue their pursuit of classic cars. In the home counties, Derek picks up two expertly crafted replicas of the batmobile. Meanwhile, Dave struggles to contain his enthusiasm for a Second World War Ford jeep.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul picks up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, Dave drools over a Ford Sierra Sapphire Cosworth, and Derek refuses a test drive in the worlds smallest production car, the 3.5 horsepower Peel P50.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story of Americas `Liberty Ships, which were produced in incredible numbers and became known as the Model T Fords of the ocean.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Machine Gun
programme.desc: No matter how many soldiers you have, how smart your generals are or how daring your strategy is, a war will not be won if there is not enough bullets, planes or food.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Trelissick House and gardens in Cornwall where the locals have dived into skips and cleared out their attics to bring along treasures in all shapes and sizes. There is a giant chair and an escape pod from a Vulcan bomber, whilst at the other end of the scale a miniature sewing kit and a gold snuff box with links to DH Lawrence prove that the best things do come in little packages.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, home to the Tollemache family for 500 years, where treasures include a dazzling sapphire ring, a Girl Guide sketch by Robert Baden-Powell and an important collection of Native American artefacts. Glass expert Andy McConnell challenges the owner of a fine collection of mid-18th-century drinking glasses to identify the lone fake, while an intricate model of a butchers shop intrigues Fergus Gambon.

programme.desc: A doting father and his 6-year-old daughter find their lives taking a dramatic turn when the woman he loves marries someone else.

programme.title: Singh Is King
programme.desc: Happy Singh is a villager who goes through a chain of misadventures that lead him to become the King of the underworld. He falls in love with Sonia, who hates criminals.

programme.title: Kasoor
programme.desc: Shekar is charged with the murder of his wife. His lawyer Simran is sympathetic and gets him acquitted. However, she starts having doubts and must protect herself from him.

programme.title: Kalicharan
programme.desc: The under trial Kalicharan is sent as Prabhakar, a cop who is his look-a-like, to arrest a rich man named Deen Dayal.
